{{Quote|We are as ghazakl worms before him. Would such a worm understand even the most menial task you perform?|Prophet [[Nom Anor|Yu'shaa]] compares the Yuuzhan Vong to their [[Yun'o|god]] [[Yun-Yuuzhan]]|<ref name="Refugee" />}}
'''Ghazakl worms''' were [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]]<ref name="Refugee" /> [[Worm/Legends|annelids]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Yuuzhan'tar]], the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the sentient [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="Worm" /> The worms were considered meaningless by the Yuuzhan Vong, and did not have enough intelligence to comprehend the most menial of tasks that the other species performed. By [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], when the Yuuzhan Vong were [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invading]] a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] other than their [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|own]], some Yuuzhan Vong still used the ghazakl worm in comparisons to make things or beings seem meaningless or unintelligent.<ref name="Refugee" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ghazakl worm was first mentioned in the [[2003]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'' by [[Shane Dix]] and [[Sean Williams]] as part of the [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series.<ref name="Refugee" /> The annelid later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]].<ref name="Worm" />
